_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites 'text' from live numbers; these are the computed fallbacks so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.cape: The Shiller CAPE ratio is at 40.7, more than double its 140-year average of 17.3 and only slightly below the infamous dot-com bubble peak of 44.19 in 1999, signaling extreme market overvaluation based on long-term earnings.
notes.buffett: The Buffett Indicator, which compares total market value to GDP, stands at a staggering 231.7%, well above the dot-com peak of 146% and the 2007 peak of 110%, highlighting that the stock market is valued at more than twice the size of the economy.
notes.concentration: Market concentration is soaring, with the top 5 companies making up nearly 30% of the S&P 500 and tech stocks representing 48%; the top 10 now account for 41% of the index, more than double their share from 2015, indicating heavy reliance on a few giants.
notes.yieldcurve: The yield curve spread between 10-year and 3-month bonds is a modest 0.55 points, while the NY Fed assigns a 31% chance of recession, suggesting cautious optimism but notable concern about economic slowdown.
notes.drypowder: Berkshire Hathaway holds a record $397.4 billion in cash as of Q1 2026, yet 79% of institutions expect their cash reserves to shrink, implying a pending release of capital or forced selling that could impact markets.
notes.finale: Between December 2026 and May 2027, major IPO lock-up expirations for big names like SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic risk flooding the market with shares, potentially draining liquidity just as cash reserves are expected to decline, creating a trigger window for market stress.
